commit.message:
[WIP] Fix httpx AsyncClient resource leak in BotApplication (#81)

* Initial plan

* fix: close httpx AsyncClient to prevent resource leak

- Add aclose() method to ConversationClient to close underlying HTTP client
- Add aclose() and async context manager support to BotApplication
- Update BotApp (FastAPI) to use lifespan hook for automatic cleanup
- Add tests for resource cleanup and context manager support
- Update FastAPI sample to demonstrate proper cleanup pattern

Fixes resource leak where httpx AsyncClient was never closed, causing
TCP connections, file descriptors, and memory to not be released in
long-running servers.

commit.message:
fix(enrichment): always rank all immediate_attention matches

Previously the top-50 budget cap excluded ~162 immediate_attention matches,
so they never got snapshots and were invisible in the matches list.

Changes:
- compute_enrichment_budget_rank: split into two passes — ALL immediate_attention
  matches are ranked first (no cap), then remaining budget slots (limit=50) are
  filled with lower-priority matches (new/defensive_gap/watchlist/stale)
- _fetch_ranked_matches / enrichment_worker: increase fetch limit to 500 to
  accommodate all immediate_attention ranks
- Dashboard card (233) and matches list will now agree once next enrichment
  cycle processes all ranked matches

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix git tree stale open feedback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: simplify Net Flow chart to single bar per week

Replace grouped arrivals/completions bars + secondary-axis line with
a single bar per week showing net = completions - arrivals.
- Green bar when net >= 0 (shipping faster than work arrives)
- Red bar when net < 0 (backlog growing)
- Dashed purple trendline on the same axis
- Y-axis label clarifies the formula: 'done − started'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs(journal): rewrite journal-list design system doc to match current implementation

The previous doc described the old FilteredList-based, date-descending
weekly view. The component was replaced with JournalDateScroll which
has a completely different architecture.

Updated sections:
- Component reference: JournalWeeklyPage → JournalDateScroll
- Date layout: ascending order with today centered on load
- Infinite scroll: top/bottom sentinel mechanics and compensation
- root:null rationale: CanvasPage min-h-screen forces window scrolling
- Scroll suppression system: how programmatic scrolls block sentinel
  loading and visible-date IO, scrollY-stability fallback, force-recheck
- Calendar navigation: smooth (in-window) vs instant (off-window)
- 'Add note' ghost row: today and future dates only
- State table: lastIODateRef replaces isScrollUpdating, dateWindow,
  suppressReportRef, mountScrollDoneRef documented
- URL params: removed ?s= (not used by this route)
- Workflow: updated to reflect scroll-up=past, scroll-down=future

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: switch portfolio to shares-based positions with live pricing and multi-currency support

Replace weight-based positions with shares-based model (ticker, shares, currency).
Portfolio percentages are now computed dynamically from live market prices,
making weights always sum to 100% and removing the need for a rebalance toggle.

Key changes:
- Add price service with yfinance integration and 24h JSON file cache
- Add forex service for currency conversion (supports fiat and crypto pairs)
- Update Position model from {ticker, weight} to {ticker, shares, currency}
- Support configurable currency list (default: USD, CAD, BTC)
- Add /api/currencies endpoint and display_currency query param on positions API
- Update web UI with currency selector, shares input, and currency dropdown
- Update pipeline to compute weights from live prices before enrichment
- Update all tests for new data model

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(fabric): use import instead of builtins for notebookutils detection (#157)

notebookutils is a regular site-package on the Fabric runtime, not a
builtins attribute. The previous getattr(builtins, 'notebookutils')
approach failed silently, causing Fabric environment detection to
always return False.

Also treat OPENAI_API_KEY='place_holder_for_fabric_internal' as absent
so the library correctly falls through to Azure / Entra ID auth.

Co-authored-by: Hiroki Mizukami <hmizukami+microsoft@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(hooks): add pre-commit gitignore guard

Detects staged files that should never be committed (logs, runtime data,
secrets, large files, compiled artefacts, etc.) and automatically:
  1. Adds the specific path to .gitignore (if not already covered)
  2. Unstages the file
  3. Aborts the commit with a clear remediation message

Pattern categories blocked:
  - Log files (*.log, *.log.N)
  - Environment/secrets files (.env, .env.*, secrets.json, config/*.key/pem)
  - Private keys and certificates (*.key, *.pem, *.p12, id_rsa, etc.)
  - Runtime data (data/*.json, data/*.csv, data/*.db)
  - Compiled Python artefacts (__pycache__, *.pyc, *.pyo)
  - Temporary files (*.tmp, *.bak, *.swp)
  - Backup files (backups/, *.backup)
  - Database files (*.db, *.sqlite, *.sqlite3)
  - Node.js dependencies (node_modules/)
  - Python virtual environments (.venv/, venv/, etc.)
  - Large files (>= 2 MB staged blob)
  - Well-known blocked paths (backend.log, config/secrets.json, etc.)

Updated install-hooks.sh to run the guard first in the composite
pre-commit hook (before secret-scan and TODO checks).

Re-run scripts/install-hooks.sh to activate.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Thematic layout to showcase-ws with taxonomy cluster overlay labels (#114)

* Initial plan

* Add Thematic (taxonomy) layout with overlay cluster labels to showcase-ws

- Add worldToScreen() and addFrameCallback() to ThreeRendererService for
  real-time HTML overlay positioning (updated at 60fps via the render loop)
- Extend TsneLayoutStrategy with getClustersWithWorldCoords() and
  getWorldPositionForId() to expose cluster regions and per-item positions
- Create TaxonomyClustersOverlayComponent that renders HTML labels on top
  of the Three.js canvas: theme-level labels at zoom out, sub-theme labels
  at zoom in (threshold: 2.5x zoom)
- Add Thematic button (3rd toggle) to the layout bar in showcase-ws
- Update switchToTsneLayout() to compute taxonomy overlay labels after
  TSNE data loads, grouping photo world-positions by taxonomy theme for
  the first level, and using server-computed cluster regions for sub-themes
- Clear taxonomy labels when switching away from TSNE layout
- Update ARCHITECTURE.md documentation

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/whiletrue-industries/future-screenshots/sessions/d41a431d-ba71-4d32-83ad-191c35bc92a7

Co-authored-by: mushon <27123+mushon@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review feedback: optimize overlay DOM caching, fix language detection, add topic format docs

- TaxonomyClustersOverlayComponent: cache DOM element references per label set to
  avoid repeated querySelectorAll on every render frame
- TaxonomyService.localizeName: make public (was private) so it can be reused for
  TSNE cluster title localization, accepts optional fields
- showcase-ws: use TaxonomyService.localizeName() instead of duplicated locale logic
- Add comment documenting the topics 'themeId/subThemeId' format

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/whiletrue-industries/future-screenshots/sessions/d41a431d-ba71-4d32-83ad-191c35bc92a7

Co-authored-by: mushon <27123+mushon@users.noreply.github.com>

* Enhance taxonomy overlay: improve fisheye handling, add fallback positioning for missing TSNE data, and refine label rendering logic

* Refactor fisheye handling: remove unused fisheyeActive property, adjust overlay rendering logic, and enhance scene rendering for improved performance

* Refactor TSNE coordinate conversion: implement hexbin layout for staggered positioning and adjust world coordinate calculations for improved accuracy

* Enhance taxonomy cluster labels: add itemCount for prominence scaling, adjust font size handling, and implement overlap culling for improved visibility

* Enhance fisheye taxonomy effects: add dynamic opacity dimming for related meshes, implement thematic focus label display, and synchronize effects with layout changes

* Implement taxonomy layout strategy: add new layout for thematic clustering, enhance label hover interactions, and optimize animation constants for smoother transitions

* Enhance typography and animation constants: add font family to filters bar and taxonomy clusters, adjust animation durations for smoother transitions, and streamline taxonomy label rendering logic.

* Enhance showcase functionality: filter out rejected grid items in output map, improve taxonomy layout with global hex grid flattening, and refine fisheye effects handling for better user interaction.

* Enhance taxonomy cluster overlay and renderer: add culling interval for label updates, implement composition margin expansion for scene bounds, and optimize fisheye effect rendering logic.

* Refactor composition margin handling: introduce default and max margin ratios, update scene bounds logic, and streamline cursor management for improved user interaction.

---------

commit.message:
feat: landmark detection, GPU fix, multi-photo pipeline, review fixes (#6)

Features:
- Multi-photo grid UI (up to 10 images), EXIF extraction, reverse geocode
- 3 model calls per photo: caption → analysis JSON → landmark (2-step
  so location_hint feeds landmark context for better accuracy)
- lat/lng stored as separate REAL columns alongside combined location string
- Card shows caption, landmark, people count, activity, objects

DB / upsert:
- INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(image_name) DO UPDATE — same filename overwrites
- UNIQUE index on image_name (created by init_db migration if missing)
- Port now passed via --port arg (single source of truth with DB_PORT in start.sh)

GPU Metal timeout fix:
- --prefill-step-size 256 for mlx-vlm (default 2048 causes timeout)
- modelCall() retries 3× with 2s/4s/8s backoff

Review fixes (Copilot PR comments):
- reverseGeocode: remove forbidden Accept-Language/User-Agent headers,
  use accept-language=en query param instead
- resizeImage onerror: reject with Error instance (was raw event)
- handleFiles: filter non-image files before processing
- buildCard: revoke object URL in img.onload to prevent memory leaks
- db_server OPTIONS 204: skip Content-Type header and body
- db_server Content-Length: catch ValueError, enforce 1 MB MAX_BODY
- db_server CORS: --allow-origin arg (default '*', can be tightened)
- db_server: port/host via argparse, not hardcoded
- start.sh: DB server readiness curl loop (was fixed sleep 1)
- pyproject.toml: real description and dependencies list

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Denver backup production + VI cluster separation + booth analytics

## Summary
Deploy full GEOINT demo to Denver Azure Local as backup production
environment, separate Video Indexer onto dedicated AKS cluster, and
add real-time booth analytics dashboard.

## Infrastructure Changes

### Cluster Separation (IoT + VI)
- IoT Operations and Video Indexer now run on SEPARATE AKS Arc clusters
  to avoid extension conflicts (IoT on den-geoint, VI on den-vi)
- Added VI_AKS_CLUSTER_NAME to env files for independent targeting
- deploy-all.ps1 auto-creates dedicated VI cluster + GPU node pool
  when VI_AKS_CLUSTER_NAME differs from AKS_CLUSTER_NAME

### Denver Backup Production
- den-geoint: AKS Arc (K8s 1.32.9) + IoT Ops + Flux + Demos 0-4
- den-vi: AKS Arc + Video Indexer + cert-manager + Longhorn + GPU
- VMs: den-geoserver (Demo 2) + den-globe (Demo 3)
- MetalLB IP pool (172.22.84.60-65) + nginx ingress on den-vi
- AAD security group (AKS-Arc-Cluster-Admins) for kubectl access

## Code Changes

### deploy-all.ps1
- Creates dedicated VI AKS cluster when VI_AKS_CLUSTER_NAME is set
- Adds GPU node pool (Standard_NC8_A2) to VI cluster automatically

### deploy-vi-extension.ps1
- Fixed cert-manager detection: checks running pods + Azure extension
  + Helm fallback (no longer only Azure extension approach)
- Fixed PowerShell variable parsing bug (EnvFile colon scope issue)

### Booth Analytics Dashboard (NEW)
- Python Flask app polling VI insights API every 2 seconds
- Real-time person detection counts from actual camera feed
- Full-screen kiosk layout: 3 hero metrics + sparkline + event feed
- Deployed as container on den-vi cluster (NodePort 30080)
- Camera registered via generateExtensionAccessToken API

### Booth Demo Script (NEW)
- 3-minute walk-up script for booth staff
- FAQ with answers, troubleshooting guide
- 6-checkbox staff training checklist

## Env File Updates
- Added VI_AKS_CLUSTER_NAME to .env.template.denver + .env.template.mobile
- Fixed VI_ACCOUNT_RESOURCE_GROUP to AdaptiveCloud-VideoIndexer

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add Quality Rate metric to dashboard

- Add quality_rate() to calculator/flow.py: % of completed items per
  week that are NOT bugs/defects. Defaults to {'bug','defect',
  'incident','hotfix'} (case-insensitive). Accepts custom bug_types.
- Add GET /api/metrics/{id}/quality-rate endpoint
- Add useQualityRate() hook to useMetrics.ts
- Add QualityChart.tsx: line chart with colour-coded points (green/
  amber/red vs 80% target), bug count bars on secondary axis, and a
  dashed purple trendline (linear regression)
- Add full-width Quality Rate panel to Dashboard below Net Flow

TDD: 17 new tests written RED-first (10 calculator + 7 API).
All 228 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(journal): eliminate scroll pop/jitter during date navigation

Four bugs were causing 'pop back and forth' when navigating between dates:

1. Initial double-scroll: lastIODateRef started as '' so ListViews
   useEffect([selectedDate]) always fired scrollToDate on mount, overriding
   the centered instant mount scroll with a smooth top-aligned scroll.
   Fix: seed lastIODateRef from the initial selectedDate so the guard fires
   on first render.

2. Sentinels not suppressed during navigation: smooth scrollToDate passed
   through the 600px sentinel preload zone, triggering prepend/append mid-
   animation. The prepend compensation (window.scrollBy instant) then fought
   the smooth scroll causing visible jumps.
   Fix: add suppressReportRef.current check to both sentinel IO callbacks.

3. Sentinels could not re-fire after suppression: IO only fires on state
   transitions, so if the sentinel stayed intersecting during suppression it
   would silently stay stuck after navigation.
   Fix: recheckSentinels() reconnects both sentinel observers after suppression
   clears, forcing IO to re-evaluate current state.

4. Fixed 1s timeout ended suppression during long smooth scrolls: the
   fallback timer could expire while a smooth scroll was still animating,
   re-enabling sentinels mid-flight.
   Fix: replace fixed timeout with a scrollY-stability poll (100ms intervals,
   release after 2 stable polls) that tracks actual scroll completion. The
   native scrollend event still handles it instantly in modern browsers.

Bonus: off-window date navigation now uses instant scroll after window
rebuild. Smooth-scrolling through a fully-rebuilt DOM had no meaningful
origin point to animate from and widened the race window for all the above.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Address review: fix maybe_update_state_size_window silent fallback, share is_soroban_key

- maybe_update_state_size_window now returns UpgradeError on missing or
  wrong-type LiveSorobanStateSizeWindow entry instead of silent Ok(())
- Make is_soroban_key pub(crate) and use it in pre_parallel_apply to
  eliminate duplicated Soroban key predicate
- Addresses review concerns from round 1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: LawnBerry MCP server with 97 developer tools

Provides comprehensive MCP tooling for the LawnBerry Pi v2 autonomous
mower project across 12 categories:

- Telemetry: live sensor data, robot status, health checks
- Missions: create, start, pause, resume, abort mowing missions
- Control: drive motors, blade, emergency stop, return home
- Navigation: GPS, map zones, geofencing, coverage planning
- Configuration: hardware config, safety limits, settings
- Diagnostics: I2C scan, serial ports, IMU calibration, self-test
- System: Pi metrics, systemd services, WiFi, disk space
- Dev Tools: ruff lint, pytest, mypy, API schema, git status
- Safety: preflight checks, interlocks, safety audit
- Weather: conditions, mowing window check
- Data: SQLite queries, log tailing, error summaries
- Docs: browse, read, and search project documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: lawnberry-mcp
repository.description: MCP server providing developer superpowers for the LawnBerry Pi autonomous mower — 97 tools covering telemetry, missions, hardware diagnostics, safety, dev tools, and more

commit.message:
Fix SVG data-flow diagram: overlapping text and arrow issues

- Widened viewBox (520 -> 560) and recentered at x=280
- Residual arrows now enter + nodes from the left edge (not into boxes)
- Added colored arrowhead markers (blue for residual-1, green for residual-2)
- Moved annotation text (x + MHA..., x' + FFN...) to right of + nodes
  with text-anchor:start so they don't overlap the circles
- Moved [B, T, n_embd] shape text below the output box instead of beside it

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: system hardening pass — warnings/errors from system check

- fix(gitignore): resolve merge conflict markers; clean up tracked files
  - Removed backend.log, backend/backend.log, frontend/frontend.log from tracking
  - Removed data/settings.json, data/ui_settings.json from tracking
  - Removed test_movement.py from tracking
  - uv.lock kept tracked for reproducible dependency pinning
  - CHAT_MODE_MODEL_SELECTION.md and other stale planning docs now ignored

- fix(health): sensor_health/overall_status rollup always returned 'unknown'
  - Python 3.11 str(StrEnum.MEMBER) returns 'ClassName.MEMBER' not the value
  - Fixed _sensor_status_to_payload() to use .value instead of str()

- fix(tls): TLS probe failed due to PermissionError on letsencrypt dir
  - setfacl grants pi user r-x on /etc/letsencrypt/live/ and archive/
  - Deploy hook at /etc/letsencrypt/renewal-hooks/deploy/fix-permissions.sh
  - Reverted openssl s_client workaround; direct file access is now primary path
  - PermissionError guards now log warnings instead of silently failing

- fix(imu): calibration_status always reported 'unknown'
  - Added _CALIBRATION_LEVEL_MAP (0=uncalibrated..3=fully_calibrated)
  - BNO085 RVC mode limitation documented; reports 'uncalibrated'/'rvc_active'
  - Fixed 'or unknown' fallback in sensor_manager to 'or uncalibrated'

- fix(tof): VL53L0X 8190mm sentinel values leaked into telemetry
  - Renamed _TOF_OUT_OF_RANGE_MM=8000 to TOF_SENSOR_MAX_VALID_MM=8190
  - Sentinel values >=8190 filtered to None in driver and sensor_manager
  - ToFData.distance_mm changed to Optional[int]=None
  - fail_count now increments (not resets) on sentinel reads

- fix(content-length): ASGI RuntimeError: Response content longer than Content-Length
  - SanitizationMiddleware returned exhausted body_iterator on large/invalid-JSON
    fallback path; reconstructed Response(content=raw) to recompute Content-Length
  - JSONResponse(status_code=304) replaced with Response(304) — JSONResponse
    renders None as b'null' (4 bytes) but Starlette omits Content-Length on 304s

- fix(navigation): nav position mismatch after GPS health recovery
  - GPS health 'unknown' caused read_gps() to return None, forcing dead reckoning
  - Added mismatch detection + logging in _update_position on GPS re-acquisition
  - position_mismatch_warn_threshold_m=5.0 logs warning when DR drifted >5m

- fix(rc-local): rc-local.service Exec format error
  - Service now active (exited) with status=0/SUCCESS

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(word): guard cardinalText/ordinalText against out-of-range levelNumber (#200)

IndexOutOfRangeException was thrown when levelNumber was 0 (z-1 = -1
array index) or >= 20000 (t1-1 >= 19 array index, past the 19-element
OneThroughNineteen array). Both cases now fall back to levelNumber.ToString()
matching the behaviour of other out-of-range formats like decimalEnclosedCircle.

Add regression tests LDef009b and LDef010b covering 0, -1, 20000, 99999.

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add LayerNorm C++ implementation blog

New building-block blog covering:
- What LayerNorm does (formula with gamma, beta, epsilon)
- torch::nn::LayerNorm usage with register_module
- Constructor options (normalized_shape, eps, elementwise_affine)
- Forward pass step-by-step (mean, variance, normalize, scale+shift)
- Learned parameters (gamma initialized to 1, beta to 0)
- Where LayerNorm lives in a Transformer (pre-norm pattern, ln1/ln2)
- Interactive bar-chart animation showing normalization in action

Links to the dedicated theory blog for background material.
Added to blog.js navigation under from-scratch/cpp/building-blocks.

commit.message:
Enrich benchmark task descriptions with file/path tokens from reference args (#109)

* Initial plan

* Enrich benchmark task descriptions with file/path tokens from reference args

Add extract_file_tokens() and enrich_task_with_files() to scenario.rs:
- Automatically detect file-like tokens in reference_args using known
  bioinformatics file extensions
- Handle bare tokens, key=value, colon-separated fields, and quoted
  shell commands
- Append missing file references to task_description as a parenthetical
- Coverage: 97.1% of scenarios now include all file references

commit.message:
fix(ci): use EsrpCodeSigning + dotnet push for NuGet (#965)

EsrpRelease@11 does not support NuGet as a contenttype — it's for
PyPI/npm/Maven/crates.io package distribution. NuGet packages must be
signed with EsrpCodeSigning@5 first, then pushed with dotnet nuget push.

New flow:
1. EsrpCodeSigning@5 with NuGetSign + NuGetVerify operations (CP-401405)
2. dotnet nuget push with the signed .nupkg to nuget.org

This matches the standard Microsoft NuGet ESRP signing pattern used by
azure-sdk, dotnet runtime, and other Microsoft OSS projects.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial release v1.0.0

WoW addon for pixel-perfect UI alignment at 4K and all resolutions.

Features:
- N-multiplier scale engine: finds the integer N such that scale × physH / 768 = N,
  giving crisp pixel boundaries without tiny UI (at 4K: N=2, scale=0.7111)
- Bypasses WoW's 0.64 uiScale CVar floor via UIParent:SetScale()
- ElvUI integration: hooks UpdateUIScale, corrects E.mult for current N
- Frame Inspector: click any frame to see pixel dimensions, alignment status,
  suggested corrections, and live gap measurement between two frames
- Alignment tools: pixel grid overlay, draggable H/V guide lines (saved to DB),
  screen centre crosshair
- Full settings GUI with diagnostics, pixel snap calculator, minimap button
- Slash commands: /pp inspect, /pp grid, /pp hguide, /pp vguide, /pp center

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: PixelPerfectUI
repository.description: WoW addon for pixel-perfect UI alignment at 4K and all resolutions. ElvUI integration, frame inspector, grid overlay, draggable guides.

commit.message:
feat: comprehensive multi-timeframe backtest with fees

- Enhanced backtester: momentum ranking entry, RR exit modes (1:1/1:2/1:3/trailing),
  re-entry mechanism, configurable bar_minutes for funding rate calc
- Full parameter optimization for 1h, 15m, 5m with quality metric
  (PF × trade count penalty to prevent small-sample overfitting)
- Multi-timeframe engine: 1h+15m+5m trend alignment at 5m resolution
  using vectorized numpy searchsorted (replaced 50M+ dict approach)
- Walk-forward validation (4 windows, train 1yr / test 6mo):
  1h:  avg OOS +357.9%, PF=1.26, 4/4 profitable
  15m: avg OOS +113.4%, PF=1.45, 3/4 profitable
  5m:  avg OOS +16.1%,  PF=1.37, 3/4 profitable
  MTF: avg OOS +18.0%,  PF=1.39, 3/4 profitable
- All results include fees: 0.08% taker + 0.05% slippage + blended funding
- Key finding: momentum ranking only helps 1h; RR modes and re-entry
  degrade performance across all timeframes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
